lines.speaker: With Minor Adjustments by
lines.text: Eric Aasen
Phoebe and Rachel's new apartment, Rachel and Phoebe are making their answering machine message.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ready?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: it's...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Rachel...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: and...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe's...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: please...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: leave...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: leave...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, I-I just said "leave."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know because you have all the good words. What do I get? I get "it's," "and" oh I'm sorry, I have "A." Forget it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, come on that's silly.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, so let's switch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I have all of the good words. OK, fine, fine, we can switch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Everybody...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Rachel...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: and...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe's...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Please...wait, how did you do that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you're no ordinary roommate are you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, come on can we finish this later? Cause I wanna go running before it gets dark. Oh! Why don't you come with me?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it'll be fun. We'll run in the park. It'll be like our first y'know roommate bonding thing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, let's go running!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah and there's really cute guys there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Let's run towards them!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, wait just one more second. Hi, it's Phoebe and Rachel's. Please leave a message, thanks!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now wait a minute. You just took all the words!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh. You've met your match Rachel Green.
Opening Credits
Monica and Chandler's new apartment, Ross and Chandler are there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, I just finished this fascinating book. By the year 2030, there'll be computers that can carry out the same amount of functions as an actual human brain. So theoretically you could download your thoughts and memories into this computer and-and-and live forever as a machine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (not amused) And I just realized I can sleep with my eyes open.
[Monica enters]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross and Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (jumps over a box) Honey, that's a great idea nailing the boxes to the floor!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't nail the boxes to the floor.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, So you can move them!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, and while I'm doing that, Ross has a great computer story for you.
[Joey enters with his new roommate who is played by none other than Elle MacPherson.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey everybody! Uh, I'd like you to meet Janine. She's-she's gonna be my new roommate!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! (Rushes over to shake her hand instantly.)

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Hi

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, she's gonna live with me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's nice to meet you. Janine...?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Lecroix. Janine Lecroix

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I didn't know that! Well, what a pretty last name!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, uh, wh-where ya from?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Australia, I just moved here a couple of weeks ago.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (shocked) From the land down under? I didn't know that either!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So uh, wha-uh, what do you do?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I'm a dancer.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're a dancer? She-she's a dancer!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well, I think I'll go and unpack.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hey let me. (Opens the door for Janine and after he closes the door behind her gasps ecstatically.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, did you even interview this woman before you asked her to move in?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Of course I did.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-huh, what exactly did you ask her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: "When can you move in?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you for bringing her into our lives.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Unbelievable

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, so you like her too Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, look at all the boxes!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ugh, I cannot wait to ask her out!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute...Joey. Joey you can't ask her out, she's your roommate. It-it'll be way too complicated.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah man don't do it. I mean if you date her, then-then-then I can't date her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, think about it. Now remember when you were going out with that girl Donna and you guys broke up. Remember how horrible it was when you guys bumped into each other at the supermarket?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh God, yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now imagine you live at the supermarket.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (happily) Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, Oh, you're right! I don't want that. I can't date her!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah and you better watch the flirting too, cause you know, in such close quarters, it could be trouble.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, that's gonna be tough Mon. I mean it's hard for me to be around an attractive woman and not flirt.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (chuckles) Hmm, well you're around me all the time and you don't flirt.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You a little sad about that sweetheart?
, The Park: Phoebe and Rachel are getting ready to go running

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I have to tie my shoe, so you go ahead, I'll catch up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (Runs off.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Starts running crazily with her arms flapping and her legs far apart) Come on! That's not running! Let's go! (Rachel pauses, then follows, embarrassed.)
, Central Perk: Rachel, Ross and Monica are there

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You guys, I'm telling you, when she runs, she looks like a cross between Kermit The Frog and The Six Million Dollar Man.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica had such a crush on him. Yeah, she used to kiss his poster every night before she went to bed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! I used to do that too!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you also have his album, It's Not Easy Being Green?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aw, Mon...(Kisses her on the cheek)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, Phoebe runs weird huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, yeah and you know what, I know she's gonna wanna run again, I just don't know how to get out of it, I mean, I live with her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why don't you just be straight with her? Tell her the truth.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're right, you're right. I should just tell her the truth.
[Phoebe enters]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, Monica tripped me, I don't think I can ever run again, ever!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why? Why would you do that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know. Rachel I'm-I'm sorry that I hurt your ankles.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ankle.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We'll see.
Monica and Chandler's new apartment, Chandler is putting something away under the sink.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OK, I'm officially unpacked. Thanks for helping me man. (Turns around and sees that Joey isn't there.) Joe? (Hears giggling coming from a box) Well, I guess Joey went home. Oh and look, there's still one box that I have to unpack. (Hears the giggling again)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (jumps out of the box) I Gotcha!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pretending) Oh my God! You-almost-gave-me-a-heart-attack.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Boy, it was so hard not to laugh, I tell ya. Hey, hey, the place looks great!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, not bad right? You know what, Monica's gonna be working late, so I'm gonna make this place spotless. You know what else I'm gonna do, know what else I'm gonna do? I'm gonna go downstairs, I'm gonna get her some flowers. Now who wouldn't wanna live with me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (raises his hand) I don't! No, I wanna live with the super-hot Australian dancer.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, now how's that going though? Are you okay with the not-flirting thing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Well, so far yeah. But it's tough you know? I got all this built up flirting energy and I don't know how to get rid of it. (Gives Chandler the "Joey-love," look.) How you doin?
Joey and Janine's new apartment, Janine is there. Joey walks in to see Janine bent over, stretching.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (voice cracking) Ohhhh man

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Sorry, there's just more room out here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no-no-no, it's-it's uh, you just uh, uh reminded me that uh, I need to do my stretches too. (Starts to stretch, groans painfully)

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: (touches his waist) Why don't you try to do-

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (jumps up and yells)

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-uh nothing. I-I-I-I didn't want you to touch me cause I'm -I'm all sweaty from the workout. I better hit the shower. (Goes into the bathroom and comes back out quickly) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Oh, sorry about that stuff hanging in there. It's just my thongs are too delicate for the dryer.
Joey laughs nervously and goes to his bedroom.
, The Park, Rachel is running and Phoebe is hiding behind a tree.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (jumps in front of Rachel) Hi! Oh yeah, uh-huh, it's me. I saw you grab your running shoes this morning and sneak out. You lied so you could run by yourself.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no Phoebe no, I was...no. You know what, I was, I was actually just checking to, see, if I could run. And I can!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Please Rachel, I am not an idiot. (Runs off)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, wait Phoebe.
, Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is there. Everything is out of its place and Chandler's cleaning.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (enters) Wow, couples who live together do start to look alike. So, Mondler...uh, what uh, what 'cha doin?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What does it look like I'm doing? I am cleaning!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did you get Monica's authorization to move all of her stuff?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Authorization? I don't need that. I'm gonna put everything back.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Put it back exactly where you found it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes I'm gonna put it back (Mocking Ross) exactly where I found it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 'Kay, first of all, this attitude is not helping.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She's not gonna care if I put her stuff back in the same stupid place.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa, hello, did you just meet Monica?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She is gonna recognize that I did a nice thing and-and, appreciate it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm -you know, actually this'll work out well. Cause when you have to move back in with Joey, Joey's hot new roommate can come and live with me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I see, I see, y-y-you're trying to freak me out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look Chandler, Monica is really weird about this kind stuff all right. Believe me, I lived with her for 16 years. She is going to freak...out. Oh my God, she's going to sit on you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, she's not okay? And I'll prove it to you. I'm gonna call her right now. (Picks up the phone and wipes it off) Phone's done ehh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (on phone) Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Mon, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Terrible. If-if I want something done right, I have to do it myself. Other people just wreck stuff. I really think I might kill someone tonight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on, come on, it can't be that bad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's worse. The only thing that's getting me through is knowing that I'm gonna be seeing you soon. I think I may even try to get out of here early.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No-no-no-no-no-no. It sounds like they really need you down there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, are you just hanging out with Ross?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's, all good! Okay bye-bye Mon! (To Ross) She's-she's gonna kill me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, the phone was facing the other way. (Chandler fixes it and a picture frame off the table.) And that goes back up there.
Commercial Break
Monica and Chandler's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We should start with the big stuff. Y'know? That'll be the easiest. Uh, let's start with the couch. (He picks up one end and Ross doesn't help) I got it. (He moves it back to where he thinks it goes.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah that-that, doesn't look right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you talking about? The couch is perfectly in line with the carpet. And then I can just walk over here and casually just put my feet right up on the...(Tries to rest his feet on the coffee table but they won't reach) OK, OK, here's what we do, we just uh, move the couch closer to the coffee table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, but then the back of the couch won't line up with he back of the carpet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OK well here, we'll just move the coffee table closer to the couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa, Whoa, Whoa, but then the coffee table won't be centered in the seating area.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but the coffee table doesn't match the...blahebdmsdlkhdyslkd;btyds...Rooosss!!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Look, what am I gonna do? I'm not flirting but still, I'm drawing her to me like - like a moth to a flame! (Tries to put his feet on the coffee table...they won't reach and looks around.) What the hell's going on over here?!?!? (Points to Chandler) Monica's gonna kill you! Look I need your help, I have to do something to-to repel this woman! Wait a minute, wait a minute, you guys repel women all the time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I've been married 3 times
Chandler gives him a round of mock applause.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no I've seen it happen, you-you get a rapport going with a woman but somehow you manage to kill it. What's your secret?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, we do not repel women OK? That is completely untrue.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no, yes we do my man. Remember when we were back in college and we went to that spring dance and you walked right up to that girl you liked and you could not stop talking about the Irish potato famine?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, well what about you? You weren't you know, so hot in college either. After everything he said, he'd go "ba dum bum chessh"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, Monica doesn't like that either, Maybe I should stop doing that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh y'know what, girls don't like it when I start talking about science.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's not specific to girls.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is great, this is great, what else, what else?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, they don't like it when you correct their grammar.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And they don't like it when you explain why your jokes are funny.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They don't like it when you keep asking them if they like you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Man, I'm so lucky I have Monica.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (nonchalantly) I'm never gonna find love again.
Phoebe and Rachel's, Rachel is there as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Phoebe, can I talk to you for a second?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, um, I...(Phoebe walks into her room.) All right Phoebe look, I just wanted to say that I'm sorry. OK? I handled the situation horribly and I should not have lied to you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, what should you have done?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I-I should've told you the truth.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-Huh, Which is...?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y'know, the reason I didn't wanna go running with you is because um, well y'know the way that you run is a little...(Starts flapping her arms)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it's embarrassing. People were looking at us like we were crazy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why do you care?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because they're people.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But people that you don't know and will never see again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, but still. They're people...with eyes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I didn't get embarrassed running next to Miss (panting). But no, okay. No, no, I can see why running with me would be embarrassing to you. Yeah, okay. You're uptight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I-I am not uptight-Hey-hey-hey-oh-oh! Listen, I am not uptight, man.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's okay Rachel. I'm not judging you; that's just who you are. Me. I'm more free y'know? I run like I did when I was a kid, cause that's the only way it's fun. Y'know, I mean didn't you ever run so fast you thought your legs were gonna fall off? Y'know, like when you were like running towards the swings or running away from Satan? (Rachel looks confused) The neighbor's dog.
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler has the tape measure out and is busy lining up the lamp on the kitchen counter, Ross is supervising the whole operation.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, is this lamp in the same place?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who cares? I repel women.
[Monica tries to come in]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (blocks the door) No-no-no-no-no-no-no-no!!! You can't come in here! R-r-r-r-Ross is naked.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (whispers) What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Ross) I couldn't say that I was naked because she's allowed too see me naked.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why does anyone have to be naked?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why is Ross naked?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I-I had to show Chandler something?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Naked?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah-yeah I uh, I have a uh, a guy problem.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is it the same thing that Chandler had?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, uh, just come later, we'll get everything squared away and you can come back later.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, listen, there's still some of Chandler's medicine under the sink in the bathroom. Bye!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye! Thank God

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dude, what'd you have?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, we have no time okay? We must focus. We gotta get everything back into its original place.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (realizes something) The photo album! There were millions of pictures of the apartment in the photo album. We just go through it and match everything to the pictures.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's perfect! That's brilliant! (Starts going through the photo album)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So really, what'd ya have?
Joey and Janine's, Janine's there. Joey enters

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Hey Joey, I got some beer for you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, don't you mean "for whom?"

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Sure, listen I was gonna order some pizza, you wanna share one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pizza, heh, its not like I never had that before...ba dum bum cheshhh.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Is there something wrong?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, All right, let's just get this out in the open okay? You're hot. I'm lovable. Clearly there's a vibe going on between us. But, we're roommates and it's a huge mistake for us to continue down this road.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Joey...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no, I'm telling ya. Imagine yourself living in a supermarket and you will understand okay? So the question is, what do we do?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well, I don't think there is anything to do. I mean I think you're really sweet, but I'm just not interested in you like that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: No! I mean you're a really nice guy and I'm happy to be your roommate and your friend, I'm just y'know, I just don't feel that way about you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! I see what happened. It's because I was trying to repel you. Right? Believe me, you'd feel a lot different if I turned it on.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I do. (Gives her the Joey-love look.) How you doin?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I'm OK

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!?!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh dear God!
The park, Rachel's running by herself and panting. After a little while she decides to run like Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Runs into Phoebe.) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey, I'm so sorry, you were right, this feels great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See? And you don't care if people are staring, it's just for a second cause then you're gone!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gone! I mean its amazing Pheebs. I feel so free and so graceful. (Turns and bumps into a mounted policeman and falls) Hey! Look out for the horse! Sorry! (Runs off.)
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler and Ross are there. Monica enters

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is it okay for me to come in now?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-huh

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why is everything different?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bye! (Runs out)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I don't see anything different other than the fact that the room got so much brighter when you came into it. (Forced laughter)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, the end table is wrong, The couch looks bizarre and don't even get me started on the refrigerator magnets.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay look, don't...don't be mad okay? But after I unpacked the boxes I wanted to do something nice for you, so, I-I-I cleaned the apartment. So I moved everything around and then I forgot where it, where it went back and I'm sorry, I'm very sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's okay. Chandler, are you afraid of me or something?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you want me to be afraid of you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course not. I mean gosh, Chandler what you did, it's, it's a wonderful thing and I really appreciate it. I know I have this weird thing where I want everything to be in the perfect place, but I'd never expect you to worry about that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh well you're the best. You come here to me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, hold on okay? First thing's first. (Gets her cleaning gloves on) Okay, now did Ross sit anywhere while he was naked?
Closing Credits
Central Perk, Ross, Janine and Chandler are there

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So it said that by the year 2030, there'll be computers that can carry out the same number of functions as an actual human brain. So theoretically we could download our thoughts and our memories into this computer...

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: ...and live forever as a machine!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's so Janine, you-you-you know what, do you know we're doing right now? You and I, we're interfacing.
